Activity.Title: Nomsa on Screen: The Missing Middle
Activity.Summary: Short film credit featuring Nomsa in a pivotal role.
Activity.DetailTitle: Nomsa on Screen: The Missing Middle
Activity.Description: A South African film student in Southern California navigates homesickness and a pivotal setback tied to an O-1 visa opportunity. Nomsa appears as the mother, bringing her Zulu heritage and warmth to the story.

Details.Label: Event Details
Details.Value: Nomsa on Screen: The Missing Middle

Images.Alt: The Missing Middle short film poster

Activity.Title: Sounds of Southern Africa
Activity.Summary: Journey through Southern African music, song, and dance.
Activity.DetailLead: SDSU School of Music and Dance presents:
Activity.Description: The band will take you on a musical journey to Southern Africa, mainly South Africa and Zimbabwe with authentic singing, dancing, featuring songs in Zulu, Xhosa, and Mbira music of the Shona people. Music that moves your soul and feet.

Details.Label: Event Details
Details.Value: Smith Recital Hall, SDSU, San Diego, CA

Images.Alt: SDSU School of Music and Dance Event

Activity.Title: Clearwater Festival 2006 with Nomsa
Activity.Summary: Newspaper ad featured Nomsa's performance.
Activity.Description: Nomsa's memorable performance at the Clearwater Festival in 2006 made it to the newspaper ad the year after, together with a picture of Pete Seger.

Activity.Title: The Diversity of Social Change
Activity.Summary: MLK Luncheon event featuring Nomsa.
Activity.DetailLead: SDSU College of Arts and Letters, Africana Studies presents:
Activity.DetailTitle: The Diversity of Social Change: Malcolm X, Martin Luther King & the Voting Rights Act
Activity.Description: Join us for an enlightening event featuring renowned speakers, including the founder of Kwanzaa. Enjoy a performance by Nomsa and stay tuned for a YouTube video of her performance on stage.

Activity.Title: Music of Southern Africa: NOMSA
Activity.Summary: SDSU World Music Concert Series featuring NOMSA.
Activity.DetailLead: SDSU School of Music and Dance presents:

Details.Label: Host
Details.Value: SDSU School of Music and Dance

Details.EventSeriesMessage: Welcome to SDSU's World Music Concert Series. A specially curated series of concerts featuring world renowned musicians who represent music cultures from around the globe. This series has been designed in conjunction with Music 345: Music Cultures of the World, but is open to all guests. We encourage you to mute your devices, open your ears, and enjoy experiencing this special presentation.

Images.Alt: Music of Southern Africa: NOMSA
Music of Southern Africa: NOMSA with audience
Music of Southern Africa: NOMSA band

Activity.Title: Mfaniseni Thusi & Friends
Activity.Summary: Südafrikanisches Sommerfest featuring Mfaniseni Thusi & Friends.
Activity.Description: Join us for the Südafrikanisches Sommerfest presented by arsNOAH Kulturverein Ismaning. Experience the vibrant music of Mfaniseni Thusi & Friends and immerse yourself in the rich culture of South Africa. Don't miss out on this unforgettable event!

Images.Alt: Südafrikanisches Sommerfest
Nomsa in Europe

Activity.Title: African Picnic Festival 2025
Activity.Summary: Nomsa with Mfaniseni Thusi & Band at the 11th African Picnic Festival in Schwäbisch Hall, Germany.
Activity.Description: A vibrant celebration of African culture featuring music, art, fashion, and food in Schwäbisch Hall, Germany. Nomsa joined the festival as part of its 11th edition.

Activity.Title: Nomsa Burkhardt at Fallbrook Library
Activity.Summary: Family-friendly performance and cultural program.
Activity.Description: For kids of all ages! Nomsa Burkhardt, co-founder of an Afro-fusion band IZINDE, is a dancer, singer, and percussionist of Zulu heritage. Performing dance, drumming, and singing, Nomsa will demonstrate her artistic skill and passion for Zulu heritage and culture.

Images.Alt: Nomsa Burkhardt at Fallbrook Library

Activity.Title: Waldkindergarten Waging
Activity.Summary: Cultural exchange through African music and dance.
Activity.DetailLead: Cultural Exchange through African Music & Dance:
Activity.Description: Immerse yourself in the vibrant rhythms and energetic dances of Africa. Have your kids join us for a cultural exchange through African music and dance at Waldkindergarten Waging. Experience the rich traditions and learn about the diverse cultures of Africa.

Images.Alt: Cultural Exchange through African Music & Dance

Activity.Title: Music en la Calle 2024
Activity.Summary: Bodhi Tree Concerts community music event.
Activity.Description: Experience the vibrant and soulful music at Music en la Calle 2024. Enjoy an evening filled with traditional and contemporary performances. Don't miss out on this amazing event!

Activity.Title: Peace Pole Rededication Ceremony
Activity.Summary: International Day of Peace community rededication ceremony.
Activity.Description: The UC Peace Pole is a 6-sided monument that displays "May Peace Prevail on Earth" in 12 different languages: English, Kumeyaay, Spanish, Chinese, South African Xhosa, Arabic, Italian, Hebrew, Japanese, French, Hindi, and Ukrainian. You are warmly invited to celebrate International Day of Peace during a community Rededication Ceremony for our beloved and recently restored University City Peace Pole.

Images.Alt: Peace Pole Rededication Ceremony
Peace Pole Rededication Ceremony
